reviews.text: Alan Swanson and his staff are considerate, knowledgeable, and trustworthy. I would recommend them to anyone looking for legal services or to be pointed in the right direction.
Great law office. The only low reviews are totally irrelevant.

Swanson Law Firm made me feel like a very valued client. Office staff is friendly, responsive and knowledgeable. I would definitely recommend to any of my friends or family. Thank you for being so great!
They don’t respond to any messages left. I left two message through their website and got nothing. I’d like to give less than a 1 star.
Swanson Law Firm greeted me and made me feel like a very important client.  They took the time to consider all my options.  Very professional and honest.  I highly recommend this firm

Called to ask a simple question regarding subject-to real estate transactions, and the possibility of this law firm facilitating the transaction, and I was quoted the hourly rate ($299.00) and told to schedule an appointment. They do not appear to be interested in developing relationships with real estate investors and agents, just out for that hourly rate... Beware!

Mr.Preble is highly intelligent and a hard worker.  He put a lot of time, and effort into helping my family. He was even willing to work on the weekend. Mr. Preble knows the law and seeks justice for his clients.
Mr. Preble is a great lawyer, and his staff is friendly and professional.
Gary is awesome, helped us navigate cps and their deceitful tactics!
Real professional
We got my step daughter back after a 7 year fight. Thank you Gary for all your support

No matter what the outcome of the case would have been, I would have always given five stars. The outcome of our case did turn out very well. If we didn't have Gary and his team it would have turned out very different. I owe it all to everyone at the Preble Law Firm. Just be prepared to answer with a yes or a no. Gary really likes his questions answered with a yes or no. I am the type to try and elaborate on something. That took some getting used to. Gary has good intentions. If you want your case to be handled by the best then I would hire Gary and his team. I truly believe it was their expertise that got us the outcome we received. He will tell it like it is. He will also crack jokes to lighten the mood. When you are going to fight a case it's best to have someone be honest with you. I will forever be greatful to Gary and his team. He is the lawyer you want on your side. He is mindful of your money too. Gary Preble and his team are the ones to hire.

When I first encountered Gary, he came off as an absolute jerk--please do not let the frocking period fool you! In time, I knew that it was his way of helping me stand strong in just about any difficult situation. He would bark questions and if they were closed-ended, you better believe that you should answer them as such or he would rattle your cage to remind you to answer appropriately. He stuck it out with me to the very end. There was even one time in which I needed his guidance on a weekend and I called, expecting it to go to voicemail, but he picked up and he helped me at the drop of a hat. I wouldn't be surprised if he decides to practice law until the day he dies, because he seemed to be living, eating, and breathing my case, sometimes late into the night or on weekends, and for that I am truly grateful. Thank you for working so hard for me, Gary!
Thank you so much with all of your help, it was a pleasure meeting you Gary and I’m so glad I found you to help with my case.

We secured Gary's service to protect our rights and our family from CPS, an agency who I found shows no respect for the law or our rights as citizens.

Normally you would think you wouldn't have to get a lawyer if you were not in the wrong. Unfortunately that is not the case and this agency threatened us and tried to manipulate us several times throughout their "investigation".

We were told they wanted access to our home to search for weapons and that we would need to answer all of their questions or risked having our four children taken from the home. This all was over my 15 year old son getting in a fight with another kid from school resulting in a black eye.

Long story short Gary shut them down with his knowledge of the law and experience dealing with their corrupt tactics. I truly feel sorry for the low income families that this CPS agency clearly takes advantage of.

Thank you Gary for protecting our family.

Gary Preble is a man of his word.  He doesn’t waste time on pointless things, but gets right to it.  I wanted an attorney with ‘Old fashion’ values - that gets right to the point - and I found him!  I was a bit surprised at first when he said ‘yes or no answers’ but I soon realized that he knew what information he needed, knew how to get that information, and wasn’t trying to waste my money with unneeded details.  Once I figured this out - I found out that Gary did what he said he would do - never wasted my time, and most definitely knows what he is doing!  His experience showed!
He hides it most of the time, but he has an amazing sense of humor and is as sharp as they get!  Five golden stars are in order!  Gary- you are the best!
Mr preble is an Amazing attorney. I had a case from CPS childcare he managed my case perfectly. He is the best attorney I ever seen. God bless you and your family Mr preble !! Thank you !!! 🌹❤️🙏❤️🌹
Got the job done

Mr. Preble is the rudest person ever! He was asking questions and I was answering them and he yelled "Yes" or "No" answers only! Said my name was uncommon..What the hell? He also said my voice would annoy the judge and he won't  represent me and I need to get it together with my paperwork. All of my paperwork is organized and the courthouse failed to stamp the documents into the system  Someone gave me his number and all I did was ask for help.  He is hideous!!
Gary really knows his CPS Cases and can help
Mr. Preble is an amazing lawyer..I would recommend him
Not available and Im in need of his help desperately😢😣😭
Daniel, Ruth Ann, & Attorney, Gary Preble conscientious, listens to your issues, & very helpful.
A wonderful attorney
Very knowledgeable, no wasting time, to the point. Perfect approach to help you with your case and to save you money where possible. Highly recommended.
Terrible customer service!! Extremely rude and degrading!

Exactly what I expect from an attorney. If I need a therapist I'll pay for one. I want someone who asks the right questions, is straight to the point and doesn't sugar coat my potential risks. I want an aggressive advocate and a real assessment. Mr. Preble offered those things and I found him to be very likeable as well.
He took the time to deal with the issues i sought advice on immediately and handled them in a professional manner. Would be happy to recommend to anyone again.
Very positive experience. Accomplished  what I needed to with no hassle.

I appreciate Al, he’s honest, fair, smart and cares about his clients. Al has always done a fine job every time I've hired him. just wanted to add I needed his help again recently with a realestate sale and he was extremely helpful and I ended up with more money in my pocket thanks to his advice. I’m happy to have Al in my corner when needed.

I was hired by Sophia Sum, the office Paralegal, to serve documents on their behalf.
Her instructions were to serve one individual in Idaho.
She did not give any special instructions, so I delivered the documents and sent her the affidavit with my invoice.
She then denied payment for my services stating that the wrong person was served.
I sent her a copy of IDAHO LAW and explained to her the serve was good.
Her attitude was extremely unprofessional, very hostile, snotty, and "know it all" which is not acceptable in any business.
She would not be employed in my business!
I am sure this bad attitude has been seen by others as well.
I doubt the "Boss" saw the 30 line email exchange, which shows her inept attitude.
It is imperative to have a professional, qualified paralegal on your staff as they typically do the preparation for the attorney.
So before you hire this company or work for them, I would think twice about the quality of work if Sophia is your contact.
Have been very helpful 🙂
Excellent legal service. Definitely recommend.
Al is extremely professional, trustworthy and knowledgeable.
Great law office with helpful attorneys that do what they say they're going to do and are really quick when needed. If you're a real estate agent and you need help these are the guys to call
I've known Al and Aaron to work tirelessly to do only work that they can stand by and be proud of. Truly upstanding citizens.
Knowledge is power.
Works diligently and quickly to accomplish client goals.  Has been a part of numerous real estate/business transactions and would recommend.

This lawyer is not compassionate at all. He is rude and would question his knowledge. He is not what i would expect of an attorney.
Chris is very down to earth polite and willing to help.
Very pleased with Christopher’s expertise and experience. He guided me as a business owner as well as for a family trust. Worth every penny!

Christopher TL Brown's Law Office in Olympia WA helped our business switch from an LLC to an S Corp. The process was streamlined and painless. He gave us great advice and helped us get to a point where we now save a bunch on taxes and also have a sound approach for gaining investment in the future. We have been working with Chris for years, for both business and personal needs. He is extremely knowledgeable and thorough when it comes to business, tax and estate planning. I trust him completely to ensure that we are making good decisions now and in the future.

reviews.text: Terrible customer service

My mother needed paperwork drawn up for estate and power of attorney purposes.  We were worried that it would be a confusing and complex process, not to mention emotionally difficult.  But Ryan and his staff were so reassuring to work with!  He spoke in a kind and nurturing way, explaining things simply and clearly.  He asked all the right questions to make sure Mom's goals would be carried through just the way she wants, even when she didn't know what kinds of documents she would need.  Her paperwork was completed in a timely manner, and it really felt like everyone at his office went above and beyond for her.  Because of how well this process went, my husband and I will soon be seeing Ryan Jacobsen to get our own paperwork in order.  It feels good knowing he has our back.  I can't recommend him highly enough!  Thank you so much, Ryan and co!

Mr. Jacobsen and his staff treated us with professional and friendly consideration.  Their handling of our recent document preparations was patient and thorough; all questions were clearly and thoughtfully answered; every email and phone call was promptly responded to; there was complete transparency about fees - no surprises; gracious referral to another firm for needed support.
We recommend this firm very highly.

reviews.text: This attorney was TERRIBLE! He botched so much with our parents estates, and would go months and months without being available or was out of the office tons. When we did finally get a hold of his office  it was only for him and his staff to tell us (in written also) that he hadn't filed papers or filed in the wrong county and was demanding a ballooned invoice payment (2 different ones, both different references and charges), for work he was going to have to redo for his mistakes, which camee only after we removed him as the attorney on record. We directed him to speak to the new attorney. This was in 2022. Today, he sends a threat email about wanting his money "or else". He became a creditor once we signed a new attorney. He didn't follow the protocol to have his bills looked at, therefore he missed the deadline for this. We owe him nothing more than he's already been paid and his abusive tactics are unacceptable. I'll be reporting him to the Bar.
Ryan has been a pleasure to work with in my Real Estate transactions.
Had a hiccup early on with getting a hold of the office, but since being in communication things have been better. Looking forward to my meeting and seeing how things go!

Ryan's a solid attorney and individual. I really enjoyed working with him on a recent real estate transaction, and he provided great service a few years ago when my wife and I had our estate plans done. What impresses me the most is how he treats everyone like equals - very friendly and not full of himself, although he really is a powerhouse of an attorney.

A few months ago I was planning on selling on of my properties without a realtor, but didn't know where to start. I consider Ryan my go-to attorney after he helped us establish a trust and addressed some old family property issues (which he resolved very quickly). When I met with him about this sale, he gave me a lot of ideas and directions to maximize my position, avoid mistakes, and warned me about a major income tax consequence (capital gains tax for about $50,000) if I didn't sell the property before November 2018.

When I had a buyer, neither of us had a realtor. So we had our attorneys prepare and review the contracts and deed, and we agreed to have Ryan's office (Jacobsen Law Office) as escrow. If I would have used a realtor, their commission would have been over $30,000! I paid less than 1/10th of that for Ryan's services and I was able to get the property sold before I lost my capital gains tax exemption in November. Altogether he saved me $80,000, so here I am to publicly thank Ryan for his awesome work!

One other thing that stood out. When there was a mixup in communications with the buyer's attorney, Ryan didn't hesitate to take responsibility for it and make it right, even though both attorneys should have acknowledged their mistake. I trust a person who is honest when it is inconvenient. Don't you?

Ryan has helped me with several legal matters and has always been such a wonderful lawyer to work with. He helped me and my husband with our estate documents and took the time to make sure we knew our options and the consequences of our decisions. When I was named as the executor of a family member's estate, Ryan (and his staff) helped me through the entire probate process from beginning to end. Ryan has a lot of knowledge about real estate and was able to resolve a problem we had with one of our investment properties. He again explained everything so we knew our rights and options, then wrote a letter that led to a settlement.

I also want to say that my close friend referred me to Ryan's law office after Ryan helped her with a special needs trust and a guardianship. She was very impressed with his work as well, and I now refer my friends and family to Ryan. I just want to thank him for all he has done for us and wish him the best.
Left a voice message.  Never returned my call.

reviews.text: My husband and I are considering filing bankruptcy which is a terrifying thought and daunting to say the least. We completed a free consultation with Erin Lane over the phone and not once did I feel judged or less than. She went over ALL of our financials and then went over our options and assured us we weren't alone. Due to the nature of our situation, filing bankruptcy at this time isn't an option, but that didn't stop her from continuing to help in any way she could. Erin gave us referrals/names/numbers of other people & businesses whom she thought could help. She even followed up with an email going over what we spoke about and a "next steps" document of what to do/not do in the coming months. If and when we decide to file bankruptcy we will definitely choose Washington Law Group, PLLC. Erin's thoughtful and well-educated consultation put our stress-filled minds at ease!

reviews.text: Says that have office in Olympia and phone number is from Olympia but that are located in Tacoma and seattSe
reviews.ownerResponse:
From the Owner:

Christopher:

First, let me apologize for the confusion. For most of the last ten to fifteen years, we have had offices in both Olympia and Tacoma, as well as in Seattle, Bellevue, and Everett. We are one of the largest and most established bankruptcy firms here in Western Washington based on total filings over the last ten years.

But, we recently had some issues with our Olympia offices space. And, for a period, we were not meeting with potential any new clients there. I am happy to report, however, that recently secured new office space.

1801 W Bay Dr NW #207B, Olympia, WA 98501

So, we still have offices in Olympia. But, the simple truth is that over the last three or four years, bankruptcies throughout Washington State have continued to be on be on a steady decline, and this is especially true throughout Thurston County.

It's great that our economy is doing well again and that people who own homes are no longer under water. But, it has presented some significant challenges for bankruptcy firms such as our that are in this practice area for the long haul. Unlike a lot of "fly by night" firms that advertised themselves as "experienced bankruptcy lawyers" during the bankruptcy boom between 2008 and 2014, Olympia Bankruptcy Lawyers is in it for the long haul. Unfortunately, however, this means making adjustments from time regarding our staffing and our availability.

As a result this latest economic renaissance, we have scaled back our debt relief staff dramatically over the last few years. This has made it more challenging to regular meet with clients at all of our offices, but especially at our Olympia offices since Thurston County bankruptcies are way down since the peak of the recession from 2010 to 2012. But, we know that many people in Olympia and throughout Thurston county are still hurting and suffering from a variety of financial ills. So, we made a decision to scale-back our Olympia presence while also remaining committed to serving Olympia.

Again, we do have new offices in Olympia and do meet with potential bankruptcy clients in Thurston County. But, in order to meet with us as quickly as possible, most of our Thurston County clients are happy to drive to Tacoma to meet with us, especially since this where their bankruptcy petition will actually be filed and where they will appear in court at their 341 Hearing.

But, again, since we have been forced cut our bankruptcy staff back in order to keep our fees low for our clients, we have been asking many Thurston County clients whether it would be possible for them to meet with us in Tacoma. There has also obviously been some confusion internally at our offices regarding scheduling potential new clients like you. And, for that, I really apologize for the confusion.

We typically only have a few days a month where we meet with bankruptcy clients in Olympia, and our staff should have offered you one of these dates, but it may have been so far out that they were just confused. Again, please accept my sincere apologies for the confusion. And, I hope that you were able to get the help you need brother.

Regards,

Erin Lane

reviews.text:
Would rate less if I could. Not only did he not come prepared for court in which we ended up losing after his “extremely confident this case will be thrown out” attitude, he expected me to takes notes for him. Didn’t know basic laws of signed documents. Misfiled a reconsideration which added almost three weeks to the trial date because their office can’t file in the correct court. Responses and explanations from office are spotty, usually late and minimal at best. Demeaning, over confident and agitated constantly. Waste of money, waste or time and resources. I would’ve been better off if I didn’t trust him with my money and confidence to perform basic tasks as an attorney.

Don’t waste your time

Edit to reflect on their response:

- The judge had to cite simple admission laws during the hearing to Stephen so he could understand why evidence was admissible and why his motion wasn’t valid. Maybe he should learn the laws, he had plenty of time to read up on them.

- The office was supposed to file the reconsideration for the case he lost since he came unprepared. Not only did his office file the motion in the wrong court calendar and had to resubmit (I assume they’ll bill me for that too) it was stricken and a new date was set which added nearly three weeks.

These are glorified process servers as that was the only thing they did right. His “advice” after losing the initial case was there wasn’t a chance to win a reconsideration and a minimal chance at best for a revision so it wasn’t worth trying for it either. Then he promptly left without further discussion.

If this is something you’re looking for give them a call, I’m sure they’d be happy to take your money only to lose a case and tell you there’s no hope after.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Andy, if you understood how the process really worked perhaps things would have been easier to accept. Also listening to the advice of legal counsel.  Essentially, Andy refused to accept the severity of his case, and denied all wrongdoings.  Despite this, the court reaffirmed its orders and denied a revision or reconsideration.  I later learned he took his own life - Rest in Peace.

reviews.text: I have been dealing with a DV case for a year now. I hired him a couple weeks ago and we went to court, we won our case. He is very to the point, doesn't small talk and stays focused. He told me upfront how much my case would cost and he didn't give me a huge bill. His paralegal is also to the point. This office allows no nonsense. Definitely recommend them for DV cases.

Steve, I just wanted to let you know that I will never forget you. You helped me when I needed to pull it together and needed to save funds because I was barely surviving while working two jobs and facing some daunting circumstances without any familial assistance. I know that you went through hell during the almost two years that I worked with you when having to deal with a great deal of turnover and for that I apologize for having to request assistance elsewhere.

I just wanted to let you know that thanks to you, we did get to the bottom of things in time. I know you were worried. You warned me about your experience with another attorney and while I know your worries were valid, I just wanted you to know that everything got settled in time. I was dealing with a battle of attrition because opposing counsel wasn't subpoenaing their witnesses. After we delt with so many continuances (most of which occurred the day of our scheduled trial), we eventually settled and the rest is history after 5 years. We stood firm and we won. I never would've held it together for those first two years without you. Thank you for everything!

Best,
Steph

When we were married over 25 years ago, I was aware that the terms of my husband's divorce from his ex had provisions for a lifetime commitment to retain a life insurance policy to protect her from loss of income from his retirement, should he die before her.
He has kept that commitment for 25+ years.  As it has turned out, we all have lived beyond normal life expectancy, and the policy that has been in effect, is expiring, and he has become uninsurable except for a very few Insurance Companys, whose rates, if paid, would reduce his income to below poverty level.
After calling some legal firms, who said we were "stuck", I talked to my brother, who was going through a divorce, and he suggested we call Pacific Northwest Family Law.
When we called, the reception was very different!!  We explained our situation and soon were introduced to Ms. Green, who understood and undertook the task of researching and developing a solution.
Our case is still in process,  and we feel confident that the outcome is going to be a much better circumstance than what we were facing.

Most cases are inherently tough, but she never backed down, no matter how complicated my case became. Her strength and commitment gave me confidence when I needed it most.
What truly set her apart was the level of amazing service she provided. Anytime I needed extra guidance, clarification, or support, she was there. She went above and beyond her standard duties to ensure I felt heard, understood, and well-prepared every step of the way.
If you are looking for an attorney who combines legal brilliance with deep compassion, I would recommend Simone every time. She secured the best possible outcome for my family, and I will be forever grateful for her hard work.

I cannot say enough good things about working with Josh. If you are looking for an experienced Olympia criminal defense lawyer, he is absolutely someone you want on your side. From the very first consultation, he was professional, knowledgeable, and completely transparent about the process.

Josh truly understands how the Thurston County courts operate and approaches every case strategically. As a criminal defense attorney in Olympia, WA, he takes the time to explain your options, answer your questions, and build a strong defense tailored to your situation.

If you need a DUI lawyer in Olympia or representation for any criminal charge, I highly recommend Josh. He is responsive, thorough, and genuinely cares about protecting his clients’ rights and futures.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the review Amanda! Greatly appreciated.

reviews.text: Dustin and his team was awesome they help me understand and made everything go smooth

I was seriously injured performing my job. I had to have spinal surgery followed up with Physical Therapy. It was very overwhelming. Then it started: requests, then demands from L&I and my employer - more aggressive and with little patience. Insult to injury. I soon realized that what they wanted wasn't in my best interest but theirs. I knew I needed help navigating this medical, now legal, situation and to get the care I needed or I was going to end up seriously financially injured as well. If this feels familiar to you, please, don't hesitate, get some legal advice. The biggest mistake you can make is attempting to deal with a legal situation vacant legal advice. Inadvertent words will make things worse. Your employer and L&I have the experience and legal advice, you really have to be equally prepared. I called Putman Lieb Potvin. They invited me to come in and explain my situation. Doubtless, one of the best decision I ever made. I am positive that without the help of my attorney, Mr. Dustin Dailey, my situation would have worsened considerably. Dustin was always straight up with me. He accurately anticipated what was going to happen and I followed his advice. L&I and my employer both treated my case more professionally because I was legally represented. I believe Mr. Dustin Dailey would ethically sort out the unfortunate condition you might find yourself in. Honest and fair, realistic and tenacious, always a professional and still my friend. He always let me know where things stood and where we were going. You won't go wrong with Dustin.

Putnam & Leib is a great law firm to work with.  My attorney,  Laura Fedorko, is absolutley amazing! She is reliable, honest and always keeps me in the loop. I am grateful to find an attorney to help me wade my way through the L&I process.  Their rates are reasonable and they are clear and open about all fees. I would recommend them to anyone in need of an honest, hard working law firm.

I have had nothing but great experiences with Desmond Law Group over the last nearly 14 years. They represented me during two dependency cases, and with their help I successfully completed a therapeutic court program. There have been many times throughout the years that I have been able to seek advice on other personal issues, and each time I was met with professionalism and solid legal advice. I am so grateful for the attorneys at Desmond Law Group.

If I could give a negative star review I would. This law office is PATHETIC. My nieces rights to proper representation has been walked all over. My family is very disappointed in this law office so much so we have put complains in with the BAR. This law office will message you non stop about being a Client but once you actually hire them they are sloppy, don’t respond to calls, text, voicemails or even emails. My advise if you need a lawyer run the other way!

Other than wanting me to "go with the flow" instead if challenging CPS allegations which I fully regret, I can't blame the lawyer, only my own decision.  Mr. Desmond is very professional and an outstanding attorney as well as his team of lawyers.

Desmond has worked closely with my husband for about 2 years for our case and he held up his end and saved our baby.... We are forever grateful to him and my own attorney Katherine Gulmert. Both of these attorneys I will HIGHLY recommend you go with 😁😁

They handled my case so well, despite how terrible every part of it was. They kept me informed of all the steps. Educated me when I didn't understand a process or what was going on. They went above and beyond anything I could have possibly hoped. I am forever grateful of the results that got me in my case. Highly recommend them.
10/10 would go to them again.
Desmond law has been impeccable with there resonce and know the law to the fullest. They have been amazing to work with,I highly recomend them to represent anyone.
Great attorney

With the help of Rebecca Sullivan from Desmond Law Group we were able to keep my teenage son out of serious trouble. She made herself available at all hours, stayed late so I could get my son in to see her without affecting his school time. She kept her composure, did her research, and came to court prepared. Without  her representation I am sure things would not have gone our way. I appreciate all of the extra effort she put in and to not have to sell the kitchen sink in order to have quality representation.

Thank you for all you did for us.

The Desmond Law Group represented us in a recent case and did an outstanding job. Chris Desmond was honest from the first consultation meeting through the end. He said our case would not be easy, but could be doable. His and their staff were always patient with us through our frustrations of length of time it was taking. The resource professionals they have available are terrific and were instrumental in our successful court ruling. Chris strategized with other attorneys representing the state and our granddaughter to maximize our position in court, calling us after hours to instruct us as to what we needed to do. His presentation in court was thoughtful, thorough and convincing. Well worth the money we spent.
Morgan Ziegler is an amazing attorney. He is very professional and prompt. I highly recommend him.

I worked with Ziegler on my account, and he was fantastic! Very clean and professional office, quick to respond to any questions i had and he made everything so simple and easy with NO stress. Will 100% recommend them for anyone needing this type of service!

Morgan helped me with a bankruptcy 6 years ago. I moved away and am now in the process of getting a home. The only thing holding me back was showing proof from court being done and over. I reached out to Morgan not knowing when I would get a response and within an hr he called me and emailed me. No questions asked he sent me the documents I needed with ease. He is truly a fantastic and caring attorney.

My husband and I cannot recommend Morgan Ziegler enough.
We had called several other attorneys that had been recommended to us by family and friends before we found Morgan.  Every one of those other calls we felt like we were being rushed off the phone,  not listened to, and that we were/ keeping the person on the other end from something more important.  From the moment we first spoke to Morgan he made us feel heard. He took time to listen to us and to talk to us,  and answered all our questions patiently and with kindness,  and made sure we understood the answers we were given before hanging up.  We instantly knew we were going to be in the best hands.  And we were.
Our first consultation was informative,  not rushed,  and eased all the concerns we had.  Morgan walked us through each step of the process patiently and with professionalism and kindness.
This is a very stressful circumstance to go through,  but with Morgan's help we can now breathe a deep sigh of relief.
Morgan Zeigler was awesome and our process was pain free....answering all our questions and walking us through the whole process. Thank you Morgan..!!!

He never made me feel like I was a nuisance for asking tons of questions, he repeatedly proved to me that he has definitely been doing this for many years. Him and his staff's compassion and understanding of individual needs blew me away. I was treated like a human, not a number. I was never rushed out during the consultation or any other appointment. They truly take the time to really prioritize your individual needs and work with you. They make sure you feel welcome and comfortable. I know how stressful bankruptcies are, and they were just smooth sailing, they handle everything, I didn't even really have to say anything during the court proceeding. Morgan said everything on my behalf and I felt amazed by how professional he is. 100% recommend. Thank you Morgan and staff.

Morgan and Star are both truly amazing! They are so helpful and patient. Especially with me! I had so many questions and they answered them in full. And very quickly. They always let me know what things I needed to turn in and if something was missing. Just a few days before the 341 meeting I had a few more questions and they responded back with what I needed to know. The day of the meeting he was in contact with me after the fact to go over what I needed to hear. And I had a few more questions and of course he helped guide me through them.
I would highly recommend going to them. If you need to file any type of bankruptcy. Definitely go and see Morgan and Star. Very professional, kind, caring, patient, and thorough with your bankruptcy from beginning to end.
Morgan Ziegler is amazing! He made the process quick and easy. Very professional and easy going would recommend to anyone looking to file for bankruptcy.
He met and exceeded every expectation I had. I recommend with without equivocation his services.

They are wonderful to work with.  Lots of respect and very helpful.  They are very helpful with questions and always made sure we knew what was going on at all times.  Definitely a wonderful office to work with while going through this tough time!

We have just finished our bankruptcy case.  I have to say that Morgan and his team were understanding, supportive and responsive from beginning to end. Starting out the process, he answered all of our questions - sometimes multiple times for the same question.  He understood that this was something we had never been through before and we were a bit scared and a lot confused.  He reassured those fears and gave very clear guidance on how to put the initial packet of information together. Once he received it, he asked for clarifying questions and made sure we forgot no account.  During the process, he was equally responsive whenever something came up: that first tax refund and how to go about declaring, when a landlord who did not file for a claim (even though he was contacted multiple times) tried to sue us for money owed, when there were pay raises,... anyway you get the idea. I never once thought my concerns were being ignored.  I even called on a Friday late afternoon once and received a return call within an hour. He and his team listened, answered questions and provided guidance and support consistently. While I hope no one has to go through a bankruptcy - if you do, you would be best served by Morgan and his team.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Katrina, you two were a pleasure to work with.  Best of luck to you moving forward.

reviews.text: Morgan Ziegler is an excellent lawyer. He is so helpful, assuring, nice and sincere. He makes you feel like you are his priority and is there to answer all your questions. His office is beautiful and his staff are amazing. His paralegal, Star, is also quite amazing. She is very nice, thorough and very helpful. I highly recommend Morgan Ziegler and his office for bankruptcy inquiries.

Support from Morgan Ziegler and Star Ramirez has been very helpful and we've learned  a ton! This could really be a good option for folks in a tight financial situation. Anxiety was immediately decreased after meeting. Thanks again!

Mr. Ziegler and his staff were very knowledgeable and helpful during the entire process. In addition, when I reached out to Me. Ziegler with questions (over a year after my discharge) he was quick to respond and answer my questions.

I don't even know where to begin but express how beyond grateful I am to both Morgan and Star. You can automatically tell and feel that they have your best interest at heart! Star is definitely a 5 star professional who was always responsive and so easy to talk to and always made you feel like you mattered. Morgan is amazing

Gosh where do I begin, well one thing is for sure that this attorney ans paralegal are so great at what they do as they are so polite and talk to you as a person. Morgan and Star are a great team I tell ya. Star was always there when we had an issue or when we were confused.  The way they treat you in a situation that isn't easy really made everything go alot smoother.  I remember exactly how I felt after i left their office the first time and that feeling was like a weight was taken off of me and my famil . The phone calls and threats become stressful and create anxiety beyond and he told me to give him the number on who's calling me right then and there at his desk (mind you was already 4pm and he had a baseball game to get to but he took care of us) and the office told the collectors to stop. I recommend anyone facing financial challenges and needing to file bankruptcy to call them, only them and DONT WAIT. They are fair with pricing and break it down exactly how they charge and again it's worth every penny and is affordable.  Years after I used them I had gotten a collection in the mail from a collector that was included in the bankruptcy but they didnt care and said pursuing next steps so I called the office and spoke to Star and she called them and all is taken care of. Gosh this office helped my wife and I start our new life stress free. Thank you Star and Morgan. Words can't describe how grateful we are with how you deal with bankruptcy. Keep up the great work and people like Star deserve a raise.

I was recommended by a friend to see Morgan and his team. You bet I did. Friend was overwhelmed and with Morgan and his team’s help of the bankruptcy process that eased off loosing property and vehicles. I called and spoke with Morgan over the telephone. Oh I wish I could have went to see them earlier. Morgan and his team are the best and very knowledgeable of helping me on the bankruptcy process. It was quick and extremely accurate process of filing bankruptcy. They listened, helpful, very friendly, pleasant and upbeat to work with. They answered  all my  questions. Morgan and team helped and relieved me from these scary and stressful moments of bankruptcy. It was the best decision I’ve ever made and saved my income that were falsely garnished and  collected by credit collectors. I wish I would have known Morgan and his team in the past years but I’m glad I went and they helped me and my family. I am sharing this review to help others that have the same issues like me.
Thank you Morgan and team for all your help. You’re awesome. May you  continue to serve and help our community. God bless.

If you’re searching for bankruptcy lawyers, I am strongly recommended Morgan & Ziegler PLLC are the best lawyers!!
Fantastic! Professional! Office staff is easy to work with and Morgan is knowledgeable and honest.

Today we are debt free and our credit survived well through this time.  I do not work for this agency.  I almost never take the time to write a review.  These people earned this review.
Fantastic communication; they were attentive and clear in communication! They set clear expectations and achieved them. They really helped me out a lot throughout the entire process

During the economic downturn in 2011 we found ourselves in a bad financial spot due to a job loss and a deceptive bank that held our mortgage at the time. After speaking to several Attorneys we decided to ask Jennie for help getting through this problem, Jennie is really good at determining the best plan to take and her and her team were able to help us resolve this issue.
Last week we received some communication about this financial issue from over ten years ago, a new company had information that was incorrect and were asking for payment on a debt that was legally resolved over six years ago, even though we thought there could not be a problem with this I reached out to Jennie, I got a call back within an hour and she spent a fair amount of time with us explaining that we had no debt and how to address this issue, and that if need be she would step in to help .
We were relieved that all is well and impressed that Jennie would take the time to help even after these years.
Jennie and her team really care and work hard for the people they represent.

Absolutely the WORST. If I could give her ZERO stars I would. I AM a current client. It took me sending emails, texts and repeated calling to get a hold of her. I called a week later to find out the status of my case, nothing. I have called, texted, emailed, another attorney that was trying to provide her information called her, she never called her back. She is the best!!! I had so much anxiety about claiming bankruptcy and she really calmed me. She explained everything to me in a way I could understand. If I ever have to claim bankruptcy again for any reason she will be the first call I make

Best bankruptcy attorney! I recommend her all the time .

Initially Jennie Patton told us to be very cautious of all other bankruptcy attorneys as they would offer to manage our bankruptcy for a very small amount and then would charge much more over the course of the Bankruptcy. Jennie Patton charged us an initial fee of $2,159.00. Over the course of the bankruptcy they charged us $8,262.62. I requested a report from them of all their charges and what the charges was for. They have not provided us with this report.
A number of years into the bankruptcy they started doing a review of the bankruptcy, this was something they did in other years. This time I questioned what they were doing. They said it was necessary to make sure the bankruptcy was on track. I asked if they were charging us additional fees for the review and they said yes. I contacted the Trustee and found out that the bankruptcy had been in a status for a number of years where no  Creditors could cause any changes to the bankruptcy. I just needed to make my monthly payments. The bankruptcy attorneys from my understanding were doing totally unnecessary work and then having to change my bankruptcy payments to cover the new attorney fee charges. I was unhappy.  Based on my experiences I would never use this firm again. I would never recommend them to anyone and have contacted other attorneys I have worked with and gave them all of these details. I firmly believe this firm is not to be trusted.

I would give them no stars if I could.
I've actually used Jennie Patton twice now for bankruptcy and both times were made to be very easy and painless. Personally I have recommended her to quite a few people.

Jennie and her team are incredibly caring, thorough, and professional.  They helped my family and I through a difficult and complicated bankruptcy for the best possible outcome.  They were supportive and never judgmental.  They were able to work around our timelines and special needs and always kept up informed at each step of the process.   For something that is going to be a horrible experience no matter what, Jennie's office made it as 'pleasant' as imaginable.  Our immense gratitude to everyone at the office.
After multiple phone calls trying to book a consultation over weeks, I get radio silence back. Even a month later. Really disappointing and frustrating that I can't even get an appointment booked.

reviews.text:
If patience were a superpower, Jamie, Kayla, and Monica would have officially earned a cape and a theme song by now. I came in with a stack of paperwork that looked like it had survived a small tornado, three cups of coffee, and only a vague idea of what I was doing. Somehow—through what I can only assume was equal parts expertise, magic, and pure emotional endurance—they managed to sort out my entire claim without once pretending to lose Wi-Fi.

They explained everything in a way even my sleep-deprived brain could understand, never judged my questionable document-organization system (“the pile method”), and checked in like a friend who actually likes me. Thanks to them, I finally feel like the VA process is something I can survive without needing therapy afterward.

If you need someone who knows their stuff, genuinely cares, and is willing to wrestle the VA bureaucracy on your behalf like it's a mildly annoyed bear—look no further.

10/10 would absolutely let them handle my chaos again.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for this review and feedback! It was our pleasure!

Of course, we are happy with the successful results of our SSDI filing through the Halpern Oliver’s Law firm.
Even if the outcome had been less successful, we would still recognize that Jamie and her staff were very diligent in their work. This took a lot of patience, and we could never have achieved the outcome without the help of this qualified law firm.

reviews.text:
I cannot thank Jamie Olivares and her team enough for the incredible work they did on my Social Security Disability cessation case. What could have been a long, stressful fight was resolved without ever needing a hearing — something I didn’t even know was possible until Jamie made it happen. My benefits were fully reinstated, and I am beyond grateful.

From day one, everyone in the office was professional, compassionate, and genuinely human. They treated me like a person — not a case file or a number, which is more than I can say for the Social Security Administration. I always felt heard, respected, and supported. They explained every step clearly and never left me in the dark.

reviews.text:
Outstanding Representation and Genuine Care

After being denied for SSDI at both the initial and reconsideration levels, I felt completely defeated and ready to give up. By the time I reached the ALJ hearing stage, the stress had taken its toll. That’s when I decided to schedule a consultation with Jamie at Halpern Olivares PLLC. Honestly, I figured if they didn’t take my case, I’d just walk away from it altogether.

At our first meeting, Jamie was understandably cautious about taking on my case. But by the end of that appointment, she agreed to move forward — and I’ll always be grateful that she saw something worth fighting for when I felt there was nothing left.

From start to finish, the entire team at Halpern Olivares PLLC treated me with compassion, respect, and genuine care. They kept me informed throughout the process and never made me feel like any of my questions were too small or unimportant. Even after my case concluded, they sent me a thoughtful get-well card during a time when I was recovering from emergency surgery. That simple gesture meant a lot — it showed me I wasn’t just a case file or number, but a person they truly cared about.

It took just under a year from hiring them to being approved at the ALJ hearing — most of that time was due to delays on the ALJ side, not the firm. Through it all, the team remained patient, professional, and dedicated.

I highly recommend scheduling a consultation with them. Even if you’re unsure, you’ll walk away knowing exactly where you stand.

Thank you again to Jamie and the entire team at Halpern Olivares PLLC — I couldn’t have done this without you.

If you need representation I would not hesitate to recommend  Halpern Olivares PLLC - Jamie Olivares for assistance.

Finding the right attorney during a crisis is not easy, but meeting Attorney Justin Huxford changed the course of one of the most difficult seasons of my life. When I first sat across from him in July 2025, I was overwhelmed and facing a civil protection order that turned my world upside down. Justin brought calm, clarity, and steady confidence from the very beginning. He listened to my story without judgment, understood the truth, and treated me with respect at every step.

Justin is a rare combination of professionalism and genuine compassion. He communicates clearly, never rushes his clients, and makes sure they feel informed and protected. Throughout the months that followed, he stood by me with patience, integrity, and unwavering dedication. I always felt that he saw me as a person, not just a case number. His guidance helped me stay grounded, and his skill made me feel safe in a very frightening moment.

Because of Justin’s expertise and commitment, the civil protection order was dismissed. This outcome lifted a heavy weight off my life and restored peace to my home. I know this victory was possible because he fought for me with both strategy and heart.

I am grateful for the way he carried himself in and out of the courtroom. He was steady when I was anxious, clear when things felt confusing, and respectful of my daughter and my faith. Working with him gave me the strength to move forward with hope.

Justin Huxford is an exceptional attorney. He is knowledgeable, trustworthy, patient, and deeply committed to protecting his clients. I would recommend him without hesitation to anyone who needs strong, honest, and compassionate legal representation. He made a lasting difference in my life, and I will always be grateful for the way he stood beside me.

Thank you, Justin. You deserve every recognition for the work you do and the lives you help rebuild.

Brian communicated clearly, made me aware of what was going on.  Brian was also very good at giving advice to help me make sure I got the best outcome for my case and not letting my emotions get ahead of me.  He was also very aware of billing and costs, and worked very efficiently.  Brian created a fair and amicable conversation around the process with the opposing party and while I can't say divorce is fun, I can say that Brian and his team were the best to work with and I am very grateful for their help.

I reached out to Bud Bay Law looking for appropriate counsel, I was paired with attorney Justin Huxford & legal assistant Olivia Drivas, They were both SO SO wonderful to work with. Their process was seamless, they gave me realistic options of what to expect, were very prompt, communicative, and caring, with their help I won my case! 5 stars is not enough! Thank you so much Justin & Olivia, I owe you and BudBay everything!

Scott Kee is the fourth attorney I hired to help my family through a very messy property dispute.  I cannot express how much I wish we had found him first. It literally would have saved years and tens of thousands of dollars wasted at other firms. He very quickly grasped the complexity of the dispute - better than any of the other 3 attorneys that were involved for months prior. He has always been straight with us. Helped us analyze our options from multiple perspectives and explained the legal side of a very emotionally charged dispute. I have zero qualms about trusting him and his advice and will continue to do so for any legal issue I may have in the future. He is honest, responsive and does what he says he will do. He never once overcharged us or sent an outrageous or surprise bill. He never missed a deadline or filing. Always kept us informed. Always took our calls or scheduled time for us.  If you’re wanting good, honest and reliable representation, Scott Kee is the gold standard.

UPDATE 2025: It has been a few years since the conclusion of the original case I mentioned above. I had the privilege of working on another, very different type of case with Scott Kee. Having an attorney that you trust is truly priceless. Scott has always given me sound advice on what my options anre and the likely outcomes through a variety of scenarios. The thing I appreciate about him the most is that no matter how emotional the situation can be, he sees it clearly through a logical, legal lens and is skilled at asking the right questions to help navigate to a successful conclusion. I always felt respected and heard. I was and am confident that he represented us as his clients well in negotiations. I am truly grateful to him for helping me yet again and for resolving the matter to the benefit of his clients.

Most people do not feel "good" about needing a lawyer, in our family's case (three) however Budd Bay not only made this not so fun process a bit easier but they are Really good people. They care about you, your family and the case. The work as a team to try to get the best results possible and the communication is above and beyond. I would recommend anyone to Budd Bay if the services lined up. We worked with Brandon, Brian and Eric. Also the backbones Dakota, Olivia and Maria... Thank you all so much.

I normally don't post reviews but am overly compelled to do this time.  I hired Scott Kee to try to recoup some money that was stolen from me by a contractor that never performed any work he was contracted to do.  Scott basically took my money and told me he was processing a lawsuit through Thurston County courts to sue this person for my money.  Well, many months went by and I never heard a word from Scott or their office except to receive constant invoices asking for more money.  When I repeatedly asked what was happening, and why I was being charged more money, but having heard nothing from them, I got attitude in return.  In fact, I'm going to copy and paste the attorney's actual quote in the message he sent me.  I was begging for their help to recover over $50k that was stolen from me.  Essentially, it seems as though they stole money from me too.  Our last interaction I asked him what to do, and his response was this, "We need to get the company personally served with the lawsuit to seek a judgment.  We tried, unsuccessfully, with the process server.  My next step would be to get a private investigator to track him down.   Open to suggestions though."
Open to suggestions?  I hired an attorney to help me through the legal process, obviously I'm not a legal expert.  So when I ask for help and an update to where my money is going, your response is "Open to suggestions."   Seriously!?!?!  Seriously!?!?!
The final straw is that when I rose concerns that my attorney was asking me for suggestions, they dropped me as a client.  Ha!  What a total joke!  Terrible at your jobs, and when you get called out you drop the client.  Now that professionalism at it's best.
Honest law firm. Very reasonable. Very ethical. Widely respected.

Budd Bay Law demonstrated exceptional expertise in handling my complex case, always keeping me informed and advocating strongly on my behalf, honest on their services resulting in a positive outcome. I was extremely impressed with Atty. Brandon Koenig together with his team Olivia Drivas for clear timely communication and ability to explain intricate legal issues in a way I could easily understand, making me feel confident throughout the process. I highly recommend Budd Bay Law no doubt about it. Chelsea at the front desk was also so kind and helpful.

For the past three years, Brandon has guided me through a parenting plan case involving a history of domestic violence. The father of my child was found guilty in a separate criminal hearing, and ensuring my little one’s safety was my top priority. Brandon’s patience, hard work, and unwavering dedication gave me the confidence and reassurance I needed during such an emotional and challenging time.

Olivia was also phenomenal—always prompt, attentive, and compassionate. Her guidance and support made the process so much smoother, even when things felt overwhelming.

If you’re facing a complex parenting plan situation, especially one involving domestic violence, I cannot recommend Brandon and his team enough. They approach their work with professionalism, care, and an unshakable commitment to their clients. Thanks to their efforts, I now have peace of mind knowing my child is safe and protected.

I recently worked with Eric Pilon and his assistant Dakota at Budd Bay Law, they were professional,  thorough, and a pleasure to work with. Eric's thorough research of the RCW's resulted in a successful outcome in court, I would definitely recommend them for legal matters.

Eric and Dakota, thank you so much for bringing peace to our family. Your support and presence have made all the difference, and we’re incredibly grateful.

Thank you for giving me my son back. I will forever be greatful to you.

reviews.text:
When I first called Ronda at Larson Law, I thought that all I needed/wanted was a simple will or update of an old existing will. I had long chosen to ignore all of the various issues that, can and often do, occur towards the later years of life.  I had been actively resistant and didn't want to deal with it. Ronda gently helped me to see and understand that perhaps it really was a good idea to deal with it.

During the process, it would occasionally take me some extended time to just process, allow thoughts to marinate, and come to terms with what I was encountering.  Ronda was patient throughout so I never felt pressured to make decisions I wasn't ready to make, and I very much appreciated that.  Ronda was easy to work with and in the end, I've come to feel that this has been a good thing to have dealt with.

Walter Smith of Smith & Dietrich Law Offices recently represented us in our claim against a national retail chain for a defective product we were sold.  After trying for several months to resolve the issue directly with the corporation, we realized we were up against the legal team of a behemoth and would need our own legal representation.  Walter Smith worked diligently and expertly from the moment he took our case, from composing the original demand letter, filing the court case and ultimately negotiating a settlement beyond what we had anticipated.  He demonstrated excellent legal expertise, provided savvy legal advice, and displayed impressive research and communication skills.  He kept the ball moving forward and provided thorough updates throughout the entire process.  We are highly satisfied with this law firm and wholeheartedly recommend Walter Smith.

We found Walter Smith, of Smith & Dietrich Law Offices to be technically-savvy, laser-focused, and highly efficient in pursuing a lemon law claim on our behalf. Not only did we get a payback that paid off the remaining car loan and refunded all the payments and interest paid from discovery that the car was a lemon, the legal fees were covered as well. He expedited an arbitration hearing and conducted deep research in preparation of the hearing, leading to a successful outcome. We consider Mr. Smith to be well-qualified in the area of consumer law and give him a five stars and two thumbs up for his support. Mr. Smith will continue to be our go-to resource for any consumer law matter.

My husband and I deeply appreciate the personable and thoroughly professional way Susan and all the staff at Cap City Law guided us through the process of creating and finalizing our Health Directive, Durable Power of Attorney and Health Care Power of Attorney.

The team at Cap City Law Firm were wonderful and helped make the process of filing my Aunt’s estate very easy. Peter and his colleagues were all very nice and professional. I would highly recommend this firm!

I went to Cap City Law for Estate Planning and I worked with Susan who was very kind and took the time to listen to my concerns. The staff were very professional and worked with me so that I could thoroughly understand the documents and carefully walked me the entire process.

I would highly recommend Susan and Cap City Law!

Attorney Susan Pai was very quick on filing everything with the courts and getting my case settled in a very timely manner. I would definitely recommend Attorney Pai for probate cases. I think Susan is a very nice lady as well.
pleasant, professional, efficient, helpful.

We hired CapCity law in Oct 2022 to address an easement dispute and health code violations.  In short, our neighbors had placed their electric meter in the middle of our well access easement, had lived on site for 10 months in unpermitted structures with no means of sewage disposal, and placed a poorly-cared-for chicken coop approximately 20' from our well head.  Attempts at reasoning with the neighbors had failed; specifically, when we asked if we could have a well driller drive around their meter to install our well the neighbor demanded we deed them a well as the price of passage -- a $26k expense.  We were attracted by CapCity's offer of a collaborative approach and hired them to negotiate a reasonable solution to the well easement issue as well as shepherd code enforcement complaints through the County.

Eight weeks later, CapCity acquiesced to the neighbor's attorney's claims that the neighbors could legally block any portion of the easement they chose so long as the one existing well could still be accessed for maintenance.  They stopped work on the code enforcement complaints midstream, issued a final bill, and walked away.  The neighbors immediately erected metal posts and chains to cordon off the remaining sections of the easement, effectively seizing it.

We hired different counsel.  He encountered a County unwilling to enforce code due to CapCity's mishandled initial complaint.  Ultimately, we were required to expend nearly $30,000 in a nuisance suit to resolve these matters.  Per our attorney, the cost would have been much closer to $75k had the neighbors not opted to sell their property and move away.

In reviewing CapCity's legal work, our new attorney's comment was, "A lot of work went into a rather useless letter and a somewhat wrongheaded analysis of the easement."  In reviewing the neighbor's attorney's positions, our new attorney's comment was, "This looks to me like he steamrolled her [Cap City Law] but failed to lock down the win with a settlement agreement."  More importantly, CapCity's legal work made it both more difficult and more expensive to resolve our claims:  "The soft-handed, soft-agreement approach of your prior counsel works against us here."

In hiring Cap City Law, we made the mistake of mixing our politics with our pocketbook.  Lesson learned.  We should also point out that the neighbor's attorney had worked for Cap City Law until just a few months prior to our hiring the firm; we have made inferences from this but will leave it to the reader to determine whether this fact bears any importance.

I have always got kind,quick, courteous, & helpful service for legal advice & paperwork 😊
Thank you Jessica ❤️
Nice personable and knowledgeable attorneys who will graciously help you with your businesses of life
For a lawyer's office it has a friendly, approachable staff from the top down...and they really know their stuff around small business in Washington state. I feel very supported. Highly recommend!
Super happy with all the work they did for us. Straight-forward, honest billing, and pragmatic.

I heard of Jessica Jensen through a woman's group and she had great reviews, so when I needed a family lawyer for a relatively straight-forward division of assets, I went to her. My main complaint is the timeliness and level of communication.  Basically everything we've asked has taken a lot longer (months) than I would have anticipated without an estimated timeline provided to us, and I've received billing notices where I feel like I need to second guess every line item for "was that really necessary?"  I would prefer to know what work is being done and the approximate cost before it is done.

I can't say that I have experience with other family lawyers, so I don't know what the norm is.  I would probably recommend Jessica for her caring demeanor and expertise, but I am likely to try a different lawyer in the future for the above reasons.
Absolutely awful experience with this firm. Not even deserving of one star. Look elsewhere if you want competent representation.
My experience with this firm was great. Excellent communication back and forth and kind and helpful advice through the entire process. Highly recommend.

Unquestionably one of the very best divorce and family law firms in Olympia and throughout Thurston County.

I have needed and used their divorce and family law legal services multiple times over the years, including a nasty custody fight and child support battle.

They did an absolutely amazing job.

Thanks again for everything Laurie!

I remember being in shock that within a matter of a few weeks after hiring Madison Law Firm, Roger and his team had accomplished more on each of my cases than my first lawyer did in 10+ months. The entire time Roger kept me informed as to what was going on. He was always on time when we had court and appeared very professional. There were a few times where I became very emotional and frustrated. Roger always kept me focused on the facts as well as what we were trying to accomplish for my daughters and I.   His team is extremely professional and well organized. Having to go through this process is not easy by any means however; I feel having the best lawyer by your side is everything and that is Roger. I would highly recommend Roger and his team to anyone who is in need of help and feeling overwhelmed in their current situation. He will give you his honest opinions and will definitely fight to help his client for the best possible outcome. I cannot express enough my sincere appreciation for all the hard work that Madison Law Firm did to help me. THANKS AGAIN FOR EVERYTHING!

reviews.text:
In 2015 I began the arduous and exhausting task of getting divorced. Buy midway through 2016 I was mired in a complicated legal struggle that included false accusations of child abuse, investigations buy CPS, restraining orders, the appointment of a GAL for my children, and wild exaggeration of my finances. At this point my attorney had back out of the case after my inability to fund his continuous loses in court. I began the difficult task of preparing for trial myself.
Feeling hopeless and at a serious disadvantage I approached Roger in the court house for advice. This was the best decision I made in the whole process. I met Roger at his office a week later just to pay for some advice. I ended up hiring Roger and with in two weeks he had made more progress on my case then myself another attorney and my X wife's counsel had made in two years.( I mean that literally.) Soon there were agreements drawn up, and a realistic conclusion was on the horizon. Despite been accused of domestic violence and child abuse I was given 40% time with my 3 young children, two week nights and every other weekend. I was order to pay child support  based on my actual income, and the division of assets was fair and equatable. I was even able to keep my home. I don't know if anyone is truly happy with the outcome of this process, but I can not imagine it working out any better.
Throughout this trying and confusing process Roger and his staff kept me informed, explained complicated details in plain language and patiently guided to a resolution that exceeded my expectations. Roger gave me sound advice and was correct in all of his predictions as to how things would play out. He understands the law and the process very well and is very adept at pushing to a conclusion with getting bogged down in the mud.
Prior to hiring Roger I had been fleeced by another attorney who lost 5 straight hearings in court and took many thousands of dollars from me and did considerable damage to my case. I then attempted to represent myself and only made matters worse. Roger was able to put all the behind me and put and end to it.
After the conclusion of my case and Roger's withdrawal he still followed up on loose ends my x wife's counsel had left and made sure all the details were taken care of. He and his staff are completely top notch, they are kind,compassionate, knowledgeable and effective. The people in his office have answered every question I asked and never left me in the dark.
For people who are going through this process without legal counsel and think they can't afford it, believe me you can't afford not to have counsel.Roger is a great lawyer, when I went to him I was totally screwed and help me more than I thought was possible. My divorce is over, I can see my children often, I can afford my child support and I kept my home.
Thank you, Roger to you and the wonderful people in your office. You're all the best.

Sincerely Steve O'Malley

Roger Madison is a very helpful and experienced attorney. He helped my children now 8, 10 and 11 to go from only 4 hours a week with me to spending 45% of their time with me, their beloved Daddy. We had gotten a bum deal when the Guardian ad Litem took the side of my ex and he recommended draconian restrictions on my children's residential time. They told the GAL that they  "just want to have both parents". Roger helped get them returned from a foreign country and fight through the ridiculous GAL report. It was a very trying experience to say the least. Roger cares about  people. His solidarity with me on this was very reassuring. Nobody wins a divorce, but my children again have both parents. They are doing very well in school and reasonably well emotionally. That is helped by the fact that I was able with Roger's assistance to be awarded my house, the children's recognized family home.

I have been a client of Madison Law Firm for about seven (7) years now.  They helped, and still are helping (representing) me through a very bitter, nasty divorce!  I am retired Army and was going through a custody battle and divorce after almost 19 years of marriage.  Madison Law Firm helped me during the toughest times in my life and helped me get through it all! I won custody of my children and got on with my life, ONLY with the guidance of Roger Madison and his Law Firm!!! They were understanding, kind, and firm in getting me the justice I deserved!  They were ALWAYS in my corner and after seven years, they STILL help me out, if "issues" from the divorce arise! If you are looking for a Law Firm that will get you the justice you deserve, use Madison Law Firm to represent you! You will NOT find a better legal team!!

reviews.text:
I cannot express enough gratitude to Ben and Jeanne at Morris-Sockle Law Firm for their outstanding support and expertise during my settlement. From the very beginning, they were incredibly compassionate and attentive, taking the time to understand my situation and needs fully. Their legal acumen and empathetic approach made me feel supported every step of the way. They were always available to answer my questions, providing clear and honest guidance that made a challenging process much more manageable.

Their professionalism, dedication, and commitment to achieving the best outcome for me were evident throughout the entire process. I highly recommend Ben and Jeanne and the entire team at Morris-Sockle Law Firm to anyone in need of exceptional legal representation. They truly go above and beyond for their clients!

reviews.text: Female clients beware!  This is a law firm that represents men in divorce.  I found that out the hard way when I asked Mr. Stookey to represent me. I was not at all  pleased with the representation I received from Mr.Matthew Stookey and Nancy Hull.  The only thing he provided was having my husband served with divorce papers.  When I went to him with questions and suggestions he would constantly tell me his opinion why my husband didn’t or wouldn’t have to do something.  It made me feel like he had my husbands best interest at heart and represented him in this case instead of me.  He was less than kind and very unprofessional and down right mean. I feel he is a misogynist.  I did everything he asked and followed his guidance or I should say lack thereof and towards the end of him representing me he went against everything he was suggesting and guiding me to do.  This is not the only time I have had to seek legal representation.  But this experience was by far the worst.  I hope for the sake of other woman, he does not represent them.  Even the front of the building says “Divorce attorneys for men”.  Please find representation elsewhere!
Much appreciated the professional, empathetic, and accessible assistance provided by Brandon, Matt, and Korey.  I would definitely recommend their services to anyone needing them.

The attorney DLFM referred us to did a horrible job and messed up our case. $40k and 1.5 years in and have only even had one hearing. We found a new lawyer who actually responds to us and doesn't let the other party walk all over them, doesn't ignore the Guardian at Litem, etc. We've emailed 3 times in the last 3 months asking Morris-Sockle to refund the $750 left of our retainer. They can send us billing statements every month saying we have $750 left, but don't respond to requests to have it refunded since we fired them months ago.

I am forever grateful for the work put in by Matt and Nancy in my case. They were professional and prepared and kept me up to date with every development throughout. Best of all, they were able to meet every goal at every hearing and get me everything I was asking for.

Thank you, Matt and Nancy!
Brandon Koening and Nancy Hull were very efficient and thorough throughout the process. Effective communication and honest conversations.

We are thrilled to hear about your positive experience with Morris Sockle Posadas. Our dedicated team is committed to providing honest and effective representation to all our clients, and we are delighted to see that Brandon and Nancy have met that goal. We are proud to have them on our team.

Your feedback is immensely valuable to us. Not only does it help others discover our firm, but it also assists us in enhancing our services. Take care!

reviews.text:
Run, run, run. This is your official warning about Morris- Sockle. You may be strained finding an attorney in the Olympia area, but turning to them will only be a waste of your money and valuable time. I was charged for work they didn’t do and conversations that did not take place. I was also charged for “busy work” by the paralegal who wasn’t even able to provide the completed form for which they billed me.

My attorney quit the firm after 2 months with almost nothing done. I tried giving this firm the benefit of the doubt and was reassigned to another attorney but same paralegal. Although the new attorney was better with communication, the overall attitude of the firm is that they own you cuz there’s not many options available in the area. Over the course of about 4 months I had absolutely nothing done, had paid over $1500 against my retainer and then they shorted me on the refund from the trust, even after I had spoken to my attorney and he said it would be handled correctly.

The Morris-sockle firm is as slimy as they come.  They are unprofessional, dismissive and you’ll even catch a massive attitude from Andrea who answers the phone. Due to how bad they were I had to reach out to the Seattle market to find decent representation. I would choose to represent myself over going with this firm because at least I can’t rob myself. You’ve been warned. Good luck on your search and Morris sockle- GFY. Your firm is clearly on the downhill hence why you have your good attorneys leaving.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Brandon,
We are sorry to hear about your negative experience with our firm, and we appreciate you bringing your concerns to our attention. We strive to provide excellent legal services to all our clients, and it is disheartening to learn that we fell short of your expectations.

At Morris | Sockle | Posadas, we value open communication and professionalism. We understand the importance of keeping our clients informed and providing quality legal services. We are sorry if you felt that the work performed did not meet your expectations or if there were miscommunications along the way. Your feedback helps us improve our processes and ensure better experiences for our clients in the future.

We take your feedback seriously and would like to address the specific issues you mentioned in your review. However, since your review is anonymous, I kindly request that you contact me directly directly so that we can discuss your specific issues and work towards a resolution. Please call the office and ask to speak with me.

Again, thank you for bringing these matters to our attention. We appreciate your understanding and the opportunity to make amends.

Best regards,
Diane Barbee
Operations Manager
Morris | Sockle | Posadas
Hi Joseph! Thank you for your high rating. Wishing you all the best!

reviews.text: I have been working with Brandon on and off over the last 4 years and he is by far the best attorney I've ever worked with. I had Brandon for my divorce, he was professional, responsive, and honest.  I never felt that his goal or the firm's was to get as much money out of me as possible; started out with consults but when he realized I needed retainer, he stopped the meeting and credited the payment for the consult towards that retainer fee, and at the end I got back what was unspent from retainer, they certainly could have found an hours worth of busy work to justify it I'm sure, but didn't.

His advice was honest, and tailored to my goals.  Even if something was legally in my favor, he made it clear what it may cost to still get that if my ex was adversarial over it.  Some may like being told what they want to hear, but I prefer the truth.  If you are going through divorce, I would highly recommend.  Saved me a lot of money and being taken advantage of.

reviews.text: I just completed a lengthy and high-conflict divorce with Paul Posadas and Kaylene Taylor as my counsel. This case had every possible twist and turn imaginable (GAL, multiple evaluations ordered by the court, lay and professional supervision, restraining order, etc.). This has been the longest and most exhausting period of my life. There was so much uncertainty and chaos caused by the opposing party, I spent many anxious nights awake wondering what fresh hell would come next. Thankfully, I knew that Paul and Kaylene had my back. I knew that Paul would keep the long term goals in mind and drive our case forward. I knew he would give good advice and explain his reasoning in a clear manner. I knew he would argue my case in a way that the judges would understand our position. I knew that Kaylene would respond to my calls and emails no matter how minor, often with a bit of professional levity that was greatly appreciated. I knew that Kaylene's eye for detail meant that nothing would slip through the cracks. I knew we would get out from under this marriage with my dignity intact. And that is exactly what happened. I highly recommend Mr. Posadas and Ms. Taylor to anyone in a complex divorce/custody situation.
