If I could go with zero stars I would...
10 out of 10 would, NOT recommend.
I paid $5,000 four years ago for Amy Perlman to help with a divorce and parenting plan.
She never met me in person to talk about anything, I sent her all my documents and she would not get to me for months..
After four years of this resending new updated documents she used all 5,000 dollars, getting me even further away from my goals.
My ex sent her own parenting plan and Amy changed it to me exs wants and needs without consulting with me first, then expected me to sign final orders that I was not ok with. (Like who are you working for, my ex, or me.. I paid for her help not my ex)
Now my ex got her own attorney and Amy decided to remove herself as my attorney now that she had used up all $5000! Leaving me and my three children in a very bad situation.

Absolutely would not recommend this office and specifically Mr Lucenko and his paralegal.  Horrible experience. Overcharged. Made multiple mistakes in favor of my ex. Paralegal hounded me while I was at work. Wouldn’t answer questions about ambiguous billing charges. If you are a woman, save yourself the frustration and find someone willing to represent YOU.
Lenny and Tammy were a pleasure to work with! The divorce process is scary and overwhelming, but with Lenny and Tammy’s help, it went smoothly and quickly. I cannot recommend them enough!!!

I can't rave enough about Attorney Kevin Meserve! He does an excellent, thorough and prompt job. His services and assistance are well worth every penny.

If you need a family law or divorce attorney, Amy Pearlman is great. She's extremely professional, combined with the perfect amount of no bullshit. Tammi, her paralegal, is wonderful. Even the front office (also Tami) was great. She's a good choice if you find yourself in need of counsel. Highly recommend.
Amy is a rockstar attorney. No one represents their clients better than Amy. She’s the absolute best.
Great 👍 attorney for all your needs. Maybe you should call 📞 them to make your life better.
They took my consult fee and then won't give me a reason why they won't consult but canceled my consult.
They were very knowledgeable and addressed my concerns and questions. Jonathan was very helpful and patient with me. I have used them in the past and are still very caring and efficient.

Representing me in my divorce, it became obvious Mr. Lucenko accepted my former husband's false allegations as truth.   My spouse had not worked 24 of our 29 year marriage and strove to fulfill years of threats that I would walk away in a divorce “without a dime".  It was a scorch earth strategy full of personal attacks and untruths.

Mr. Lucenko refused to forward my questions onto my former spouse's attorney until too late and pressured me to enter into mediation ill prepared and without my questions being addressed.  Mr. Lucenko’s paralegal told me my diamond earrings, a gift, were only half mine.  I felt defenseless.

An emotional time to be sure, only worsening with Mr. Lucenko at the helm after which I detailed my experience in a complaint to the Bar.

I encourage every woman seeking representation in a complicated divorce to go elsewhere.

We were  extremely pleased with the quality of service. Our Attourney Erin Kuns was professional,  yet personable.  We really felt she asked  all the questions that provided us with such a clear understanding of what we needed to plan our Estate and  Will needs for the future. I highly recommend  this firm and Erin especially.
Friendly, professional, and you leave with confidence.
Definitely go with these guys.

Paying Mr. Meserve for his counseling was easily the worst investment of the entirety of my family law case. Not only was he incorrect on factual details, but when corrected he treated me like I was trying to lie to him. These weren't even contentious details, things you'd expect someone to lie about. These were raw, citable, facts.

Mr. Meserve's guidance when he saw I was accused of [Insert Crime], was to "plead guilty". This was a family law case, not a criminal case. My case couldn't reasonably be taken to criminal court because the only evidence is the testimony of my clearly hostile ex-partner. Which either makes me a criminal mastermind or there wasn't smoke, because there wasn't a fire.

Had I of taken Mr. Meserve's guidance, as he had taken my money, I would now be criminally liable. Now, a year later, the court found that my partner was clearly being deceptive and attempting to leverage the system in a predatory way. Yet that doesn't change the potential degree of damage the guidance from this lawyer could have caused.

If you have limited funds, like most of us do and are in a vulnerable place dealing with a system that is supposed to represent you, but is equally and seemingly purposefully made so complicated that you have to hire someone to decipher it. I would certainly not risk spending your money on this gentleman.

My husband and I finalized our wills and other documents yesterday. This is the firm we used. It was literally a short phone call, a couple emails, and a 15 minute in person noterizing appointment. It could not have been easier and we got not only our wills but power attorney docs and such taken care of as well. Highly recommend!

Chuck Szurszweski and the entire team at Connolly Tacon & Meserve (CTM) helped my family more than words can say.  If you have any need for family law representation call Chuck at CTM.  He changed my case (and my life) around so I had a chance.  My daughter is grateful and so am I!

This company will charge you so much in interest far more than you actually ever paid. Then sell your debt to other agencies to continue stealing.   It’s quite scandalous. Chris Meserve is a rock star attorney.  No one represents clients better than Chris.

After my motorcycle accident, they worked quickly to gather evidence and negotiate a fair settlement.

Made everything easy and stress free during a difficult time.

Look no further than Hugh! He is professional, sharp as a tack, and on top of it lawyer! My case (that was interwoven with 3 of my colleagues at the same time) he dominated!  He answered questions, walked us through the entire process and stayed factual.  I would recommend him to anyone and have done so!  I don't know why my last review was removed but, I wanted to make sure to place one again.  I can't say enough great things about this man.  He had my back, and won my case (and all my colleagues cases too!!)

Over the past year I had the good fortune of being represented by Hugh in an employment dispute which had left me a shadow of a man.  From our first conversation it was clear Hugh is a uniquely empathetic person, someone who meets you not just as a supremely capable professional but as a fellow human being.  Were it not for his humor, his grace, and his kindness, there is no doubt in my mind this process would have pushed me past my breaking point.  Prior to this engagement I had zero experience in civil litigation; by the time Hugh successfully wrapped things up I truly felt like a partner with agency in the process.  I do not use the phrase "Good People" lightly.  Hugh is Good People.  Hugh is also an *excellent* attorney, and as an attorney myself I was beyond impressed with his knowledge and expertise.  I don't know what I did in a past life that merited the good fortune of getting to work with Hugh but it must have been a doozy.  I cannot recommend Hugh highly enough and will be forever grateful for his tireless efforts in our successful quest for justice

I had the pleasure of working with Hugh McGavick, and I am incredibly impressed with the quality of representation I received. From the outset, Hugh demonstrated exceptional expertise and dedication to my case. Every aspect of my legal matter was handled with utmost professionalism and attention to detail.

Timeliness and responsiveness were top-notch throughout the process. Hugh was always available to address my concerns and answer my questions promptly, which made me feel valued and reassured. His ability to communicate complex legal concepts in an understandable manner was particularly impressive and greatly appreciated.

My overall experience working with Hugh was outstanding. His compassionate approach and unwavering commitment to achieving the best possible outcome for me made a significant difference. I felt supported and confident throughout the entire process.

The result obtained exceeded my expectations, thanks to Hugh's strategic thinking and relentless pursuit of justice on my behalf. I couldn't have asked for a better advocate.

I wholeheartedly recommend Hugh McGavick to anyone in need of legal assistance. His exceptional skills, responsiveness, and dedication to his clients set him apart. I am grateful for the outstanding service I received and would not hesitate to seek Hugh's counsel in the future.

Hugh took on my case with short notice and explained what steps I needed to accomplish for my trial. The lawyer cost was fair and saved me thousands of dollars due to to winning vs an old employer appealing my unemployment benefits.
He is super patient and flexible. Easily won my case and I appreciate all his hard work and knowledge. Will definitely use him again if needed (but hopefully I won’t have to…)

He kept me well informed and was very realistic with all possible solutions and outcomes for my case. I felt that he truly cared and I was very thankful he was willing to represent me and he resolved my case exactly how I had hoped.

I truly can't thank him enough.

The best thing about Hugh is that his experience provides this sense of relief because he always explained the law and how it applied to my case.

Anyway, thank you Hugh! Wishing you the best and I'll be in contact with you shortly regarding our ongoing matters! :)

My experience with Hugh McGavick came through the Unemployment Law Project, where they provide pro bono legal aid. Hugh McGavick is a stone cold junk yard dog. He personally saved me $$$$$. The unemployment offices are coming after small fish to recoup after they had millions stolen by other countries through fraudsters. Most people resign and accept judgement. Not me. I hired Hugh back when he was part of the U.L.P.
Nowadays, Hugh represents the best of us trying to earn a living while the powers that be skim off the top. If you are in a financial bind due to unemployment injustice, Hire Hugh.
His retainer is worth it’s weight in gold. Hugh is calculating, methodical, and has the tenacity to get justice. I will hire Hugh any day if the week.
Don’t roll over! Fight to keep what little our governments provide.
They aren’t giving you housing, food, healthcare or education, but you can be sure you’ll keep what you’re due, and PAY HUGH.
My case hearing was very successful and I am thankful to Mr. Hugh Mcgavick because he cared about his clients and was very helpful. Im am very glad he handled my case.

I began working with Ostrander & Deuel, back in 2020, after filing my claim and undergoing surgery.  I contacted their office  because I didn’t fully know what the next steps were in the L&I system. Their office helped me navigate my claim with ease and understanding.   It was very helpful having them as my representative during the complicated vocational rehabilitation process.   In the end, they helped me finish my claim with the best possible result. The entire office was very easy to work with and I couldn’t ask for a better experience.

Dayl
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Dayl! It was so great working with you and I'm so glad we could help you!

reviews.text: If it were not for Breanna I would have never got pension hands down she will put in the work for you. She is TOP notch at what she does.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Manny! It was so great working with you and we are so happy we could help you obtain your benefits!

reviews.text: I sustained a severe back injury, while on the job, and this firm helped me from the beginning to the end of the claim.   I could not have managed my claim without representation.  I highly recommend this firm.   Even if you do not think you need an attorney, you should have your claim reviewed, because the Department of L  & I does not always handle your claim appropriately.  These attorneys and their staff are very professional and did a great job handling my claim.
They were kind and compassionate and worked hard on my case

I called on October 7th 2021,to see if they could help, with an L&I claim. They sent paperwork to fill out, I then dropped the paperwork in there drop box, office doors locked due to Coronavirus. Didn't hear back so I called a few times no answer. Then I get a letter from L&I saying since you haven't proved worsening of your condition your appeal denied. I was dumbfounded cause I never even spoke to anyone in this law office about my claim. I wasn't aware they even filed an appeal. Not a very professional law firm.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Vince, I'm so sorry you feel this way. Just to clarify a few things - we did receive the paperwork that you filled out which is paperwork we have to have to be able to begin accessing your claim file for review. Part of that paperwork includes you signing a document that we fax into the Department to preserve issues that could be appealable while we take the time to review the claim. This was done, as standard procedure, so that could be what you're talking about with your appeal. There might have been an Order in your claim that was already protested, and therefore the protest we sent in that you signed was to what's called an appealable only order. The Department is the one that sends that on to the BIIA.

Further, after we received your paperwork we informed you that we needed you to go into your claim file to allow us access. We returned all phone calls that were made and you could not enter the lobby because our lobby was closed between September and December of 2021. Unfortunately, without access being granted to us by the injured workers in claims, we cannot access your file because it's confidential to you. After a month of not being able to reach you and not having access to your claim, we closed our file assuming you were no longer interested.

Again, we are sorry you feel this way but feel free to reach out to us to discuss this issue.

reviews.text: Breanna represented me and did a fabulous job! I would definitely recommend this firm for anyone having to navigate the L and I system. They are detail oriented, great with communication and don't make you feel like just another case number. I can't say enough good things about WWO!

Breanna worked endlessly on my claim to ensure each aspect of the claim was correct. I had constant communication either with Breanna or her paralegal Julie. Both ladies are knowledgeable, thorough, and will stay on top of the Department of L&I to make sure everything is done correctly. I can not thank these ladies enough!
Great knowledgeable attorneys, I have seen them in action for years. They will represent you with absolute professionalism and competence
Had an appointment, when I got their the door was locked and I had to call to get in.
Great place
They was nice to talk to on phone they was in to what my daughter had to say sorry about other comment
reviews.ownerResponse: This review obviously is not for our law firm.

reviews.text: I got injured at work in 2016, and getting Dane Ostrander and this firm to represent me in my L&I claim was the best decision I ever made. Before I had them on my side L&I was fighting me at every turn and closing my case, now I have a bright new future ahead of me! I really can't express how grateful I am for all the hard work these people have done for me :)

They represented my appeal with WA L&I. They did it at no cost to me. I ended up in a better position than when they took my case. Communication was great even in a Covid-19 environment. Everything was wrapped up in a couple of months. I would recommend them.

Dane Ostrander and his firm represented me in my LnI claim and always made me feel like I was the only client they had, and they listened to my concerns.  They were very understanding and supportive. They were able to help me get a good sized PPD and get vocational retraining.

From 2007 to 2012, Dane Ostrander and his firm represented me for my Labor and Industries (L&I) claim. Dane has an indepth understanding of L&I's processes and procedures. Without his representation and guidance, I'm sure I would have lost my case. My case wasn't easy to win. My former employer submitted written objections to L&I, my doctor's sent mixed messages, and my young age (47 years old) played a role. Still, I was unable to work fulltime due to my health issues. Dane fought hard and smart and eventually we won my pension. Payment for Dane's services was taken over a period of time from my L&I pension which kept my income from suffering a big hit. I've recommended Williams, Wyckoff, and Ostrander  to friends and family. It's time to recommend them to others, as well. Tom Patch
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your review.  We very much enjoyed working with you and look forward to helping others with their L & I claims.

reviews.text: They do work for their clients and they're always friendly.
Unfortunately they could not help me with my case.  However they were very nice and did give me some good advice.

I would recommend this Law Office to anyone needing a L&I Attorney. After a 3 and a half year battle with L&I and two bad decisions from the board, Wayne won my case in a jury trial. The jury even awarded my Attorney fees. Every in the office is extremely helpful and polite.
